meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve can't deploy apps (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't deploy apps. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve Deploy Issues
content.seo_description: Overcome deployment blockers by mastering professional portfolio building. A high-level checklist for students using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Deployment failure is often a symptom of poor architecture, not just infrastructure. By treating your portfolio as a production-grade SaaS, you force yourself to master CI/CD, environment variables, and scalable hosting. Use Antigravity to automate your infrastructure and Claude Code to iterate on deployment logic until your app is live.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect logic, only to watch your terminal vomit a wall of red text the moment you attempt to push to production. That phantom 'deployment wall' is the rite of passage for every senior engineer, but for a student, it feels like a terminal career blocker. You are not failing to code; you are failing to engineer the environment.

Transitioning from 'scripts that run on my machine' to 'apps that run on the web' is the single most important delta in a developer's career. By shifting your focus toward professional-grade portfolio building, you stop guessing at deployment and start architecting for reliability. Let’s dismantle the deployment fear using the most advanced AI-assisted tooling available today.

steps.title: Containerize Your Environment
steps.description: Eliminate 'works on my machine' by encapsulating your app in a Docker container. This ensures parity between your local environment and your cloud provider.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready Dockerfile for a Node.js/Python hybrid application. Include multi-stage builds to minimize image size and add a .dockerignore file for security.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a Dockerfile that optimizes for cold-start performance'

steps.title: Define Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
steps.description: Stop manual clicking in cloud consoles. Use Antigravity to define your infrastructure, allowing you to recreate your entire stack with a single command.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a terraform configuration to deploy a scalable web server on AWS, including security groups and an S3 bucket for static assets.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Review my terraform provider setup for security best practices'

steps.title: Automate CI/CD Pipelines
steps.description: Configure GitHub Actions to automatically test and deploy your code upon push. This builds the discipline of continuous integration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, unit tests, and deploys to a staging environment on every pull request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a .github/workflows/deploy.yml file for automated testing'

steps.title: Implement Observability Patterns
steps.description: If you can't see why it crashed, you can't fix it. Integrate structured logging to track production health.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a structured logging middleware for my API that captures request latency, error codes, and stack traces.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add Winston logging to all express routes'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: The final push. Use Claude Code to execute the deployment script and verify the live endpoint health.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a shell script that checks if my production endpoint is returning a 200 OK status after deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute deployment script and verify site health'

pro_tips: Always use environment variables for secrets; never commit them, even in private repos.
Implement a 'staging' environment that mirrors production exactly to test migrations.
Use health-check endpoints that return 200 only when database connections are established.
Master the art of 'graceful degradation' so your app remains functional even if a microservice fails.
Treat your infrastructure code with the same rigor as your application code—version control everything.
content.next_steps: Once your app is live, focus on setting up automated monitoring and error alerts. Then, refactor your code to improve performance by 10% based on production metrics.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student writing code; you are an engineer shipping products. Take these habits, apply them to your next build, and watch your portfolio transform from 'homework' to 'high-impact'. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
